question: Deep knowledge of statistical tests used to analyze genomic data (e.g. NGS, GWAS, single-cell sequencing)
Deep knowledge of one or more programming languages to process and analyze ‘omics-level data
Deep knowledge of genomic data file types and formats
Critical evaluation of scientific and medical literature
‘Omics-relevant statistical analysis
Distributed cluster / cloud computing, & working with big data (i.e., running pipelines using UNIX / command line)
Validating informatics software
Bioinformatics pipeline creation / implementation for ‘omics analysis (e.g., WES, WGS, RNASeq, Bisulfite-Seq)
Data visualization for assessment of mapping and variant call quality (e.g., using a genome browser)
Technical laboratory skills in genetics / genomics methodologies
Laboratory safety
Tolerance / acceptance of uncertainty and ambiguity
Keeping a lab book & documenting lab work
Writing for medical professionals
Writing clear, tactful, and professional emails
Generating and using slides effectively (e.g., PowerPoint, Keynote)
Speaking clearly, effectively, and persuasively
Effectively and succinctly synthesizing / summarizing complex information to generate insights
Creating visual communications (e.g., posters, infographics, figures)
Leveraging social media platforms to communicate science
Training and mentoring individuals
Seeking advice and asking for feedback / clarification from advisors and mentors when needed
Demonstrating workplace etiquette (e.g., punctuality, consideration, flexibility, gratitude)
Complying with rules, guidelines, policies, laws, and regulations
Upholding commitments and meeting deadlines
Working effectively in a hierarchical team (e.g., collaboration, sharing timely updates, willingness to take on tasks for the good of the project / team, reporting to a team lead / supervisor)
Managing / leading a team, and / or working with direct reports
Planning, organizing, establishing priorities, and time management
Adapting to changing goals, projects, and circumstances at work
Creativity / innovative and strategic thinking
Problem solving and critical thinking
Working under pressure / managing tight deadlines
Building and maintaining positive relationships with colleagues, and an encouraging, supportive, and psychologically safe workplace culture
Managing conflict and conflict resolution, building consensus, compromising, and exercising tact and diplomacy in the workplace
Listening / being attentive
Providing constructive feedback
Working with and adapting to the needs of patients / clients / stakeholders / community members from diverse and varied populations
Understanding data ownership, sovereignty & sharing issues
Understanding privacy and disclosure issues in the context of medical genetics
Demonstrating accountability for one’s actions, and a dedication to acting in accordance with the highest standards of professionalism
Designing and / or querying ‘omics-level databases
Designing analysis pipelines for ‘omics data
Using code / pipelines written by others, with no (or with minor) modification, general maintenance of existing pipelines
Writing new code, or optimizing / heavily modifying existing code
Installing, testing, and debugging bioinformatics software for ‘omics data analysis

Analyzing and interpreting experimental (pipeline) results
Performing wet lab experiments / following protocols
Designing new wet or dry lab experiments, techniques, protocols, and / or experimental tools
Performing research with pre-clinical samples (e.g., rodents, cell lines)
Performing research with human samples (e.g., tissue, blood, swabs)
Performing clinical research / trials involving patients
Variant filtering, prioritization, and triaging from ‘omics data
Critically reading scientific and medical literature
Using in silico tools for variant assessment
Writing position papers or policy papers
Creating digital content (e.g., newsletters, social media posts, podcasts, webinars, websites)
Creating presentations
Providing technical guidance on the application of products and policies related to genetic services
